attribution: California Department of Fish and Wildlife, California Recreational Fisheries Survey ds3185; accessed through the California Open Data Portal and CDFW ArcGIS service.
denominator: Surveyed anglers whose primary target fell within the broad CRFS Bottomfish trip category in a block and period; one angler means one person fishing in one block on one day. This is not species-specific effort for any rockfish, cabezon, greenling, or lingcod taxon.

field_semantics.rule: The eight period-specific All_* and Kept_* fields use -9999 where that bin has no published value. Preserve it in the raw snapshot, translate it only to an explicit missing value in derived data, and never interpret it as negative catch or zero.

license.note: The portal does not state a version number on the dataset page; do not infer one. Preserve attribution and re-check the current portal terms before redistribution or commercial use.

permitted_uses.reason: The layer pools multiple caught taxa into RCGL and many targets into Bottomfish effort, lacks complete attempt rows, and supplies only block/time-bin support.

query.pagination: OBJECTID-ordered resultOffset pages of at most 2000
query.transport: approved curl capture plus offline fail-closed verification

sampling_design.kind: published aggregate CPUA by CRFS one-minute BlockBox
sampling_design.multi_block_rule: When an interview reported multiple blocks, catch was divided across all indicated blocks.
sampling_design.release_floor: Only blocks with at least three reported fishing trips over 2004-2024 are published.
sampling_design.source_transition: 2004-2015 records came from the PSMFC legacy RecFIN database; 2016-2024 records came from the CDFW CRFS system.

spatial_support: CDFW CRFS 1-minute BlockBox polygon. It is an aggregate area, never an exact fishing point; no centroid may be converted into a catch location.
temporal_support: Aggregate bins 2004-2009, 2010-2015, 2016-2020, 2021-2024, plus the full 2004-2024 period; no exact event time.
